projects.title: Sideline
projects.summary: Sideline is an in-progress, user-centered web app for youth soccer families to upload, organize, share, and collaboratively tag game clips.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A collaborative youth sports platform focused on clear workflows, family coordination, and scalable product direction.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A MERN architecture with JWT auth, media pipelines, and team-oriented clip metadata flows for scalable implementation.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: An easier way for youth soccer families to upload game videos and find the moments that matter.
projects.problem: At the top of the funnel are parents recording youth soccer games for memory keeping, sharing, and review, but current workflows are fragmented and existing tools do not fit their specific needs.
projects.observation: Research indicates this audience is primarily white millennial mothers who need a simple, mobile-first flow for team coordination, game uploads, and finding meaningful moments inside large video sets.
projects.hypothesis: A minimal, familiar interface paired with team-based video workflows and key-moment tagging will reduce friction from recording to replay while improving collaboration among families.
projects.experiment: Defined a MERN architecture where React frontend and Node/Express backend communicate via JWT-authenticated requests, MongoDB stores application metadata, S3 stores video assets, and AWS Lambda handles video manipulation pipelines before returning processed clips to users. Current implemented scope includes wireframe/wireflow, mobile-first frontend layout, secure login/user creation, and initial backend video-query foundations; next steps are deeper frontend playback integration and expanded backend video systems.
projects.outcome: The project is still in active development, so there is no final product outcome yet. Current progress establishes validated UX direction, core account flows, and foundational data/video architecture for iterative testing.
projects.reflection: The strongest early learning is that this product succeeds only when complexity stays behind the scenes: login, dashboard, team/game navigation, invite flow (QR/join code), and crowdsourced clip tagging must feel effortless for non-technical parents.

projects.title: Stackd
projects.summary: A mobile-first web-based flashcard app with custom decks, no screen clutter, UF-specific features, and Quizlet migration assistance.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A focused ed-tech app aimed at reducing study friction and improving retention for University of Florida students.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A MERN flashcard product with JWT auth, structured deck models, and low-friction review workflows.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A simple flashcard app that helps students study faster without clutter or confusion.
projects.problem: Existing flashcard apps are designed for everybody rather than specific user needs, which can be overwhelming and often lack flexibility in deck creation and organization.
projects.observation: UF students in memorization-heavy courses want a simple way to create and review flashcards without being forced into complex features or rigid structures.
projects.hypothesis: A minimalist flashcard app with intuitive deck management that can improve their study experience.
projects.experiment: Building a MERN stack web app with React frontend, Node/Express backend, MongoDB for storage, and JWT authentication. Features include deck creation, card editing, marking cards, and course-based organization.
projects.outcome: Future delivery of a functional flashcard app that balances simplicity with effective study techniques, allowing users to focus on learning rather than app management.
projects.reflection: Educational tools succeed when they minimize friction and cognitive load, letting users engage with the learning material directly.

projects.title: P2P File Sharing Application
projects.summary: A peer-to-peer file sharing application built with a custom TCP protocol, supporting direct transfers without central servers.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A networking-focused build demonstrating distributed systems thinking and protocol design.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A custom TCP-based peer protocol for decentralized direct file transfer with no central relay dependency.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A tool that lets people send files directly to each other without a middle server.
projects.problem: Existing file sharing apps are often complex and require a central server for management and distribution.
projects.observation: Users want a simple and efficient way to share files directly without relying on central servers.
projects.hypothesis: A peer-to-peer file sharing application with a custom TCP protocol can provide a more efficient and decentralized solution.
projects.experiment: Building a P2P file sharing application with a custom TCP protocol, supporting direct transfers between users without central servers.
projects.outcome: Future delivery of a functional P2P file sharing application that simplifies file sharing and reduces dependency on central servers.
projects.reflection: Decentralized applications can offer more control and efficiency for users, reducing reliance on central infrastructure.

projects.title: NightLink
projects.summary: Dream journal and social network with AI-generated dream titles/summaries, privacy controls, and social interactions.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A differentiated social product balancing retention mechanics with explicit privacy controls.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: React + Firebase social journaling stack with AI proxy integration and granular visibility states.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A dream journal where you can keep entries private or share them with others.
projects.problem: Most dream journals are private-only and low-retention, while social apps are not designed for reflective dream logging.
projects.observation: People want to keep some dreams private, share others selectively, and still get meaningful interpretation support.
projects.hypothesis: A hybrid product combining private journaling, social feed controls, and lightweight AI analysis increases long-term journaling consistency.
projects.experiment: Built with React 18 + Vite, Firebase Auth/Firestore, and an AI proxy endpoint. Added private/public visibility, tagging, following, profiles, comments, reactions, and CI/CD with GitHub Actions + CodeQL.
projects.outcome: Delivered an end-to-end journaling platform with social mechanics and maintainable quality gates (lint/build matrix + security scans), ready for iterative UX experiments.
projects.reflection: NightLink validated that privacy granularity and social features can coexist when visibility and identity controls are explicit in the product model.

projects.title: Frequify
projects.summary: Windows desktop mastering tool for transparent, adjustable audio processing with local offline workflows.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A creator tool emphasizing trust, clarity, and offline reliability for independent audio workflows.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A .NET desktop DSP pipeline with analyzers, configurable mastering chain controls, A/B playback, and WAV export.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: An offline audio app that helps creators master their tracks with easy controls.
projects.problem: Independent creators often need mastering improvements without opaque black-box tools or online-only processing.
projects.observation: Users care about understandable controls, A/B comparison, and dependable local exports more than novelty presets alone.
projects.hypothesis: A desktop-first mastering workflow with analysis metrics and explicit chain controls improves trust and practical output quality.
projects.experiment: Implemented WAV/MP3 loading, loudness/peak analysis, mastering chain controls (EQ, compression, saturation, stereo, limiter), original/mastered A/B playback, and WAV export in a portable Windows build.
projects.outcome: Shipped a fully local app with straightforward install/run flow, offline operation, and clear troubleshooting for common audio and export failures.
projects.reflection: For creative tools, controllability and transparency are as important as DSP quality—users need to hear and understand each adjustment.

projects.title: Nexel
projects.summary: Strategic 9x9 block placement puzzle with solver mode, combo scoring, drag-and-drop UX, and local high-score persistence.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A polished game product that combines replayability, strategy depth, and educational AI-assisted modes.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A React/Vite game loop with stateful board logic, solver strategies, move validation, and local persistence.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A Block-Blast-like puzzle game where you place blocks, score combos, and can compare your moves to AI.
projects.problem: Many puzzle implementations lack strategic depth visibility and do not expose decision quality beyond final score.
projects.observation: Players benefit when game feedback explains placement validity, combo opportunities, and survivability trade-offs.
projects.hypothesis: Adding solver strategies and real-time placement cues can make a minimalist puzzle feel deeper without adding UI clutter.
projects.experiment: Built a React + Vite game with 30+ shape patterns, row/column/3x3 clearing, combo multipliers, move validation highlights, game-over detection, localStorage highscores, and Greedy/Survival/Hybrid solver autoplay modes.
projects.outcome: Created a fast, dependency-light strategy loop where users can both play manually and inspect AI decision styles for learning and replay value.
projects.reflection: Simple mechanics become compelling when feedback loops are immediate and strategy trade-offs are surfaced clearly.

projects.title: Pulse Gear
projects.summary: Third-person Unity puzzle game where you play a sentient bulldozer in an abandoned factory, and the only way out is to use your pulse mechanic to blast obstacles out of the way.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A playable Unity prototype showing strong mechanic iteration and player-feedback driven tuning.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A C#/Unity physics puzzle prototype centered on pulse-force systems, cooldown tuning, and staged level progression.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A game where you control a bulldozer and solve factory puzzles using a pulse blast ability.
projects.problem: Many puzzle games lack a satisfying tactile interaction model and do not provide insight into solution strategies.
projects.observation: Playtesting showed players responded most to the heavy movement feel of the bulldozer and the instant environmental response when pulse force hit debris, but they struggled when feedback on pulse range and timing was unclear.
projects.hypothesis: If traversal, pulse charge, and obstacle reactions are tightly telegraphed through camera framing, VFX, and audio cues, players can solve spaces through experimentation without needing text-heavy instructions.
projects.experiment: Built the game in Unity with a third-person controller, physics-driven obstacle interactions and a staged factory room that increases puzzle complexity throughout the level. Iterated on pulse cooldown, knockback force, and obstacle mass tuning to keep challenge high while reducing dead-end frustration.
projects.outcome: Shipped a playable vertical slice where players navigate the factory, charge their pulse, use pulse it to clear routes, and experience a cohesive movement-combat-puzzle loop with clear progression and retry flow.
projects.reflection: The biggest lesson was that puzzle difficulty should come from decision-making, not control ambiguity. In Unity projects like this, readable feedback loops (charge state, impact direction, and cooldown clarity) are what make a mechanic feel both satisfying and fair.

projects.title: Splitsy
projects.summary: React Native expense-splitting app with OCR receipt scanning, group management, and cross-platform workflows.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A practical fintech collaboration app validated with user testing and measurable task-time improvement.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A React Native + Node/Mongo architecture with JWT auth and OCR microservice integration for receipt parsing.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A mobile app that scans receipts and helps groups split bills fairly automatically.
projects.problem: Manual bill splitting is slow and error-prone, especially for recurring group expenses and itemized receipts.
projects.observation: Users need one flow spanning scan → assign → split → track, rather than disconnected calculators and chat messages.
projects.hypothesis: OCR-assisted input with shared group context reduces completion time and improves perceived ease versus manual splitting.
projects.experiment: Built mobile app (React Native + Expo) with Node/Express + MongoDB backend, JWT auth, friend/group management, transaction CRUD, and Python Tesseract OCR microservice. Conducted a 10-participant within-subject evaluation.
projects.outcome: Study results in project documentation showed task time reduction (~33 seconds) and higher satisfaction (~88.3/100) compared to manual methods.
projects.reflection: Cross-platform collaboration apps succeed when technical complexity (OCR, auth, multi-service setup) is hidden behind a tight user flow.

projects.title: ProducerFingerprint
projects.summary: Local-first producer style analysis app that extracts harmonic, rhythmic, spectral, and microtiming fingerprints from multiple tracks.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A creative analytics tool translating complex audio signals into understandable producer-style reports.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: An Electron + Python signal analysis stack using Librosa/music21 for multi-track feature extraction and reporting.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: An app that analyzes songs and explains the production style behind them.
projects.problem: Producer identity is usually discussed qualitatively, making style comparison difficult to reproduce and test.
projects.observation: Creators want a practical report that blends measurable DSP features with human-readable interpretation.
projects.hypothesis: An Electron + Python hybrid can combine a lightweight desktop UX with strong audio-analysis tooling to produce actionable fingerprint reports.
projects.experiment: Implemented drag-and-drop multi-track import, chromagram/key/chord extraction, BPM/rhythmic density metrics, spectral tilt analysis, microtiming detection, dashboard visualizations, and JSON export with configurable analysis parameters.
projects.outcome: Produced an offline-capable analysis workflow that turns raw audio batches into structured, explainable style profiles.
projects.reflection: Creative analytics products work best when advanced processing is configurable but the default path remains one-click clear.

projects.title: 15 Slide Puzzle
projects.summary: Web-based 4x4 sliding puzzle with multi-tile movement, animations, and optimal-path A* solver mode.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A dual-purpose game and learning experience that increases engagement through transparent algorithmic feedback.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A browser puzzle implementation with state validation, A* search, and step-by-step solution playback controls.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A slide puzzle game that can also show the best solution path step by step.
projects.problem: Most slide puzzle demos focus only on gameplay and do not expose solver reasoning or state navigation for learning.
projects.observation: Users enjoy experimentation when they can test custom board states and inspect each solution step.
projects.hypothesis: A solver UX with validation, step-by-step playback, and jump navigation increases educational value and replayability.
projects.experiment: Built drag interactions for single/multi-tile moves, solvability validation, A* pathfinding, step timeline controls (prev/next/play), and direct step-jump state inspection.
projects.outcome: Shipped a puzzle experience that works as both a game and an algorithm visualization tool for search strategy understanding.
projects.reflection: When algorithm output is made interactive, users trust and learn from it more than from static result text.

projects.title: GatorFound
projects.summary: University-focused lost-and-found platform for secure, private item recovery with authenticated campus users.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A campus trust-focused platform addressing real community coordination and safety needs.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A React web app with authenticated posting/comment workflows tailored to university identity constraints.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A secure campus app for reporting lost and found items and connecting with owners.
projects.problem: Campus lost-and-found communication across social media is fragmented, public, and inefficient for matching rightful owners.
projects.observation: Users need a trusted centralized channel with identity checks and direct communication around item claims.
projects.hypothesis: Restricting access to verified UFL email accounts and consolidating lost/found posting improves trust and recovery outcomes.
projects.experiment: Built a CRA-based web app with authenticated posting for lost/found items, comments for coordination, and campus-specific onboarding via UFL email requirements.
projects.outcome: Established a privacy-focused hub with core recovery workflows and a clear roadmap (admin tools, filtered search, notifications, user dashboards).
projects.reflection: Community trust features (who can join, who can post) are foundational product decisions, not post-launch add-ons.

projects.title: Gridiron Guru
projects.summary: NFL situational recommendation system over 538,745 play-by-play snaps (2013–2024), available as web dashboard and C++ console app.
projects.shortSummaryRecruiter: A large-scale analytics product combining systems performance and approachable decision-support UX.
projects.shortSummaryEngineer: A dual-interface analytics stack using C++ data structures and later integrated with React/TS over a shared 500k+ play dataset.
projects.shortSummaryNonTechnical: A football analytics tool that helps choose better plays using a huge historical dataset.
projects.problem: Raw play-by-play data is too dense for fast, situational decision support without structured analytics and retrieval.
projects.observation: Different users prefer different interfaces: interactive visuals for exploration, terminal tools for direct repeatable queries.
projects.hypothesis: Combining modern frontend exploration with performant core data structures improves both accessibility and analytical control.
projects.experiment: Implemented React + Vite dashboard, TypeScript CSV parsing/analytics utilities, and C++ max-heap/hash-table console mode operating over a unified dataset pipeline.
projects.outcome: Delivered dual-mode analysis workflow with a shared data source that supports rapid exploration and deeper systems-oriented experimentation.
projects.reflection: Data tooling reaches more people when UX and systems performance are treated as complementary, not competing goals.

personalLiveSignals.currentlyInto: Human behavior systems, interactive storytelling, and audio tooling

personalInterestSections.title: Curiosities
personalInterestSections.intro: Themes I keep coming back to across creation, interaction design, systems, and human behavior.

items.title: Music & Creative Interfaces
items.subtitle: Creation, agency, and flow over passive consumption
items.imageAlt: Music and creative interface curiosity placeholder
items.description: I enjoy how music creation feels active and immersive compared to passive listening.
details: I keep studying how agency and flow state change the experience of making something.
I care a lot about making creative tools intuitive and fun.

items.title: Experimental Interaction & UX
items.subtitle: Memorable interfaces, tactile patterns, and perspective-aware design
items.description: I care about experiences that feel fun and stick in memory.
details: I'm constantly exploring where to balance simplicity and depth.
I'm especially interested in personalization and how it changes perception.

items.title: Nostalgic / Physical / Vintage Tech
items.subtitle: Why older tactile devices feel more satisfying
items.description: I really like old tech that feels physical and real to use.
details: I care about how texture, feedback, and physicality shape user emotion.
I see tech as expression, not only utility.

items.title: Systems Thinking & Optimization
items.subtitle: Modeling real systems and finding inefficiencies
items.description: I like finding weak points in systems and figuring out better ways to run them.
details: I'm curious about the relationship between individual agency and system constraints.
I'm drawn to problems where better coordination creates large gains.

items.title: Psychology & Human Behavior
items.subtitle: Perspective, memory, authenticity, and cognitive limits
items.description: I'm interested in what makes experiences feel personal and authentic.
details: I often think about nature vs nurture and where human limits come from.
I keep exploring why creation feels fundamentally different from passive consumption.

items.title: Media & Personal Interests
items.subtitle: Music, films, collectibles, and rotating obsessions
items.imageAlt: Media and personal interests curiosity placeholder
items.description: I like curating music, movies, vintage clothing, and whatever I'm currently into.
details: This section rotates over time as new interests become recurring themes.
Right now the strongest overlap is between AI, UX, and creative tooling.
